title: ExtractedFields
description: Ground-truth field set for a single PDF in the mock-document corpus (one PDF = one ExtractedFields payload). Field names align with corpus/schema/extracted-fragment.schema.json where the concepts overlap (notably `travelers`); fields here use snake_case per tech-spec sec. 2.2 and capture the printed text on the document rather than normalized engine values (e.g. city names as they appear on the PDF, not IATA codes).

isoLocalDatetime.description: ISO 8601 local datetime as printed on the document: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS`, no timezone designator.

placeEntry.description: Shared core shape for structured place arrays (`stations`, `accommodations`, `venues`). Each concrete array composes this with a bucket-specific datetime fragment via `allOf` + `unevaluatedProperties: false`, and narrows `kind` via its own enum. The cross-field rule that `city` must appear in the document's top-level `cities[]` is enforced by the validator code, not by this schema.

city.description: City name as printed on the document. Must conceptually match an entry in the document's top-level `cities[]`; integrity is checked by the validator, not by this schema.

kind.description: Subtype of the place. Each array narrows this to its own enum (see the concrete array's `items.properties.kind`).

identifier.description: Stable identifier as printed on the document - IATA code for airports, printed name otherwise.

stationDatetimes.description: Optional departure / arrival datetimes for a transit station. An origin has departure only; a terminus has arrival only; a layover or return-leg turnaround has both. Either or both may be absent; the validator enforces per-document-type presence rules.

accommodationDatetimes.description: Required check-in / check-out datetimes for an accommodation entry.

venueDatetimes.description: Optional valid-from / valid-to datetimes for a venue entry (e.g. timed-entry sightseeing slot, parking validity window).

document_type.description: Kind of travel document. Snake_case mirror of the engine fragment schema's `documentType` enum (e.g. `air_ticket` here corresponds to `air-ticket` there); `airbnb_booking` and `supplementary` are PDF-corpus-specific extensions.

cities.description: City names exactly as printed on the document (e.g. "Paris", "Lisbon"). Distinct from the engine fragment schema's `cityCode` IATA codes - the corpus captures the printed surface form so PDF extraction can be evaluated literally.

stations.description: Transit endpoints printed on the document (airports, rail stations, bus terminals). May be empty for documents that don't carry this bucket (e.g. a hotel booking). Per-`document_type` minimum-count rules (transit >= 2) and per-entry datetime-presence rules are enforced by the validator, not by this schema.

identifier.description: IATA code for `airport`; printed station name for `rail_station` / `bus_terminal`.

accommodations.description: Lodging entries printed on the document (hotels, Airbnb properties). May be empty for non-lodging documents. Per-`document_type` minimum-count rules (lodging >= 1) are enforced by the validator, not by this schema.

identifier.description: Printed property name (e.g. "Hotel Lutetia", "Airbnb - Loft in Alfama").

venues.description: Other places of interest printed on the document (sightseeing, parking, miscellaneous). May be empty.

travelers.description: Traveler names as printed on the document. Matches the `travelers` field in corpus/schema/extracted-fragment.schema.json.

prices.description: Prices printed on the document. May be empty.

qr_codes.description: Raw payload strings of every QR code visible on the document. May be empty.

pdf_kind.description: Whether the PDF carries a real text layer (`text`) or is image-only (`rasterized`). Drives the validator's per-PDF sanity check and the runner's path-mix summary.

scenario_id.description: Stable scenario identifier. Layer 1 uses the generator's `NNN-<slug>`; Layer 2 uses any stable contributor-chosen string (e.g. `porto-trip/02-hotel-booking`).

noise_seed.description: Seed used by the generator's noise layer. Layer 1 only; absent for Layer 2 scenarios.
